posts.text:
Oil Tanker Booked in Gulf at 897% of Benchmark Freight Rate

 A supertanker has been provisionally booked to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at a rate equivalent to almost nine times benchmark freight costs, an eye-watering price that reflects the shortage of
posts.summary: A supertanker has been booked to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at approximately 897% of benchmark freight rates, indicating an acute shortage of available shipping capacity in the tanker market. This dramatically elevated shipping cost reflects significant supply-demand imbalances that are increasing the overall cost of crude oil transportation.

posts.text:
Hormuz traffic cautiously improves

Confirmed Strait of Hormuz traffic stayed active on 23 June, with 31 verified crossings across commercial and energy-linked vessels. West-to-east movements dominated, while Iranian, Omani and IMO routes all remained in use. The Strait appears https://t.co/TOe354A89q
posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz traffic showed signs of stabilization on June 23 with 31 verified vessel crossings, suggesting continued flow of energy supplies through this critical chokepoint. The activity across multiple shipping routes indicates that oil and energy shipments are moving without major disruptions.

posts.text:
EIA inventory report:

U.S. commercial crude inventories fell by 6 million barrels, while the Strategic Petroleum Reserve declined by a further 9 million barrels to a fresh multi-decade low. Combined, total U.S. crude stockpiles have fallen by around 100 million barrels since https://t.co/YYb4R7ie68
posts.summary: U.S. crude inventories have declined significantly, with commercial stocks down 6 million barrels and the Strategic Petroleum Reserve hitting multi-decade lows, creating tighter overall supply conditions in the oil market. The combined 100 million barrel reduction in total U.S. crude stockpiles suggests reduced cushion against supply disruptions and could support higher oil prices.

posts.text: Trump is now turning his attention to refiners and fuel producers, criticizing the slow pace of declines in retail gasoline prices following the sharp drop in crude oil. However, the refining sector's response remains constrained by historically low inventories of refined https://t.co/qPgOM4srBS https://t.co/lyha5bPb9A
posts.summary: Trump is criticizing fuel producers for not lowering retail gasoline prices quickly enough despite recent crude oil declines, though refiners' ability to respond is limited by historically low inventories of refined products.

posts.text: WTI crude oil $69.96/bbl... if this was fall/autumn we'd eventually see the national average $3.25/gal- but RFG/summer gasoline specs and strong crack spreads will mean for now the national average might eventually drop into the mid-$3s. a sub-$3 run is possible around November.
posts.summary: WTI crude oil at $69.96/barrel could push the national gas average into the mid-$3s currently due to summer fuel specifications and strong crack spreads, with potential for sub-$3 prices by November. The seasonal shift from summer to winter fuel blends is a key factor that will affect future pump prices.

posts.text: For just the third time ever, PADD 2 saw weekly back-to-back refinery utilization rates over 100%
posts.summary: PADD 2 (the Midwest refining region) recently experienced back-to-back weekly refinery utilization rates exceeding 100% for only the third time on record, indicating exceptionally tight refining capacity and strong regional fuel demand. This rare operational intensity suggests limited spare capacity in the region and potential upward pressure on refined product prices.

posts.text:
Hormuz traffic cautiously improves

The Strait of Hormuz continued to show signs of operational recovery on 23 June, with 31 verified crossings recorded across a broad mix of vessel types, including #crude, chemicals, container, bulk and general cargo traffic. West-to-East https://t.co/ZIsVhezO9j
posts.summary: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz showed signs of recovery on June 23 with 31 verified crossings of various vessel types including crude oil carriers, suggesting improved operational conditions for global oil transport through this critical chokepoint. The resumption of crude oil flows through Hormuz could help ease supply concerns in international oil markets.

posts.text:
Oil investment of $17.7 trillion required to meet demand in the long term.

Total cumulative oil investments to meet demand requirements add up to $17.7 trillion
(US$2026) for the period 2026–2050. For the upstream, investment requirements are assessed at $14.5 trillion. https://t.co/lvRY7jPW3r
posts.summary: OPEC estimates that $17.7 trillion in cumulative investments will be needed between 2026 and 2050 to meet long-term oil demand, with upstream operations requiring $14.5 trillion of that total. The projection underscores the substantial capital commitments required to sustain global oil supply over the coming decades.

posts.text:
Global energy spending is on track to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026

Around $2.2 trillion of this is set to go to grids, storage, low-emissions fuels, nuclear, renewables, efficiency & electrification

And about $1.2 trillion goes to oil, natural gas & coal: https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/9SrWhBJOx8
posts.summary: Global energy spending in 2026 is projected to allocate approximately $1.2 trillion to fossil fuels including oil, natural gas, and coal, representing about one-third of total energy investment. This indicates that despite significant growth in clean energy spending ($2.2 trillion), fossil fuels continue to command substantial capital investment globally.

posts.text: EIA: Miserable report for refiners with 3.1 m build in distillate stocks and 2.1-m build in gasoline. Gas demand measurement of 8.775-mbd will restart demand destruction dialogue. But refiners, blenders and importers are no longer bringing 10-mbd of gas to market, so be careful.
posts.summary: The EIA report showed weak refinery conditions with significant builds in distillate and gasoline inventories, while gasoline demand at 8.775 million barrels per day signals potential demand destruction. With refiners and importers no longer supplying 10 million barrels of gasoline to market, the supply-demand imbalance could create market pressure.

posts.text: Share prices for two of America's largest gasoline retailers, $MUSA and $CASY, are down 5-6 percent this morning after President Trump threatened to use the Department of Justice to investigate gasoline prices.
posts.summary: Major U.S. gasoline retailers Murphy USA and Casey's General Stores saw share prices decline 5-6% following President Trump's threat to use the Department of Justice to investigate gasoline prices. The market reaction suggests investor concerns about potential regulatory pressure on fuel retailers.

posts.text: Russia’s Moscow Oil Refinery may remain offline until 2027 after suffering extensive damage from Ukrainian drone strikes. The facility normally supplies nearly half of Moscow’s fuel needs, adding pressure to a domestic market already facing shortages and rationing. #Russia
posts.summary: Ukraine's drone strikes have damaged Moscow's Oil Refinery extensively, potentially keeping it offline until 2027 and disrupting the supply of nearly half of Moscow's fuel, likely exacerbating existing fuel shortages in Russia's domestic market.

posts.text: Norway’s crude oil production beat official forecasts by 7.2% in May, reinforcing its role as one of Europe’s most reliable energy suppliers. Despite lower output versus April, year-to-date petroleum production remains 4% above 2025 levels. #Norway #Oil #NaturalGas #EnergyMarkets
posts.summary: Norway's May crude oil production exceeded forecasts by 7.2%, demonstrating its continued reliability as a European energy supplier, with year-to-date output running 4% ahead of 2025 levels despite a sequential decline from April.

posts.text:
If only things were that simple.

First, 'Big Oil' no longer operates fuel retail stations.

Then, the prices you see at the pump reflect all this. https://t.co/vzxkzxRaBc https://t.co/uFobnmkd2b
posts.summary: Oil and Gibbs note that major oil companies no longer directly operate retail fuel stations, and that pump prices already reflect the complex dynamics of the oil market. The implication is that simplistic explanations for gas prices ignore this structural separation between upstream oil production and downstream retail operations.

posts.text:
Any attempt to protect legacy businesses is like trying to hold back the tide, just basics of creative disruption. The truth is that oil futures have always been and will always be a grand casino and perps is just a new cool game in town.
#oott

https://t.co/rPyPy1cK5Z
posts.summary: Bouchouev argues that protecting legacy energy businesses is futile against disruptive innovation, while characterizing oil futures markets as inherently speculative with perpetual futures (perps) representing an emerging trading mechanism. The post suggests structural change in the oil market is inevitable and that new financial instruments will continue to reshape how oil is traded.

posts.text:
East Asian refiners seek sanctions clarity before buying Iranian crude

🔷 South Korea, Japan, Thai, Taiwan refiners seek sanctions clarity
🔷 Refinery configuration adjustments needed for Iranian crude

This uncertainty could limit Iranian oil demand in a key regional market despite price incentives.
